response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with genuine geospatial exposure through GISCollective and Green Map, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position demands deep, production-grade C++ or Rust for high-performance routing data pipelines; his C++ use is limited to a 6-month NI role in 2014–2015 and a university thesis, with the past decade spent almost entirely in TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. He has no routing tile pipeline, navigation guidance, or transport-network geospatial experience — his GIS work is web-based map collection and visualization, a meaningfully different subdomain.
strengths: Geospatial domain exposure via GISCollective, Green Map GIS, and a FOSS4G 2019 conference talk demonstrates genuine familiarity with spatial data concepts
Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience at Sociomantic (async I/O, fibers, high-performance databases handling millions of req/s) shows systems-level thinking
Linux proficiency and DevOps fluency (Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD) align with the production infrastructure requirements of the role
concerns: C++ experience is limited to a single 6-month commercial role in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis; no modern C++ in over 10 years and zero Rust experience
All geospatial work is web-based GIS data collection and visualization — no routing tile generation, navigation pipelines, or transport-network data processing
Career trajectory is firmly in TypeScript/Node.js/web engineering; the reskilling gap to low-level systems C++ for a routing pipeline is substantial

key_factors.factor: No routing or navigation pipeline experience
